title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Tea Horse Road (Yunnan → Tibet)
description: Kunming Yunnan — start of the 1,400-mile overland caravan route from the 7th century. Compressed tea bricks were the only form that could survive months on yak caravans.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Tea Horse Road (Yunnan → Tibet)
description: Lhasa Tibet — caravans took 3–6 months to cross passes reaching 16,000 ft. Tea became essential nutrition for Tibetan highland diet.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Maritime Silk Road (Fujian → Europe)
description: Fuzhou Fujian — source of the 'té' word-path. Maritime traders from coastal China used the Min word 'té' which spread by ship.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Maritime Silk Road (Fujian → Europe)
description: Canton (Guangzhou) — sole port Europeans could trade through under the Qing Canton System until 1842.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Maritime Silk Road (Fujian → Europe)
description: Malacca Strait — chokepoint for all European tea-bound shipping.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Maritime Silk Road (Fujian → Europe)
description: Colombo Ceylon — Dutch then British port hub.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Maritime Silk Road (Fujian → Europe)
description: Amsterdam — Dutch East India Company first imports tea to Europe in 1610.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Maritime Silk Road (Fujian → Europe)
description: London — by 1750 tea taxes fund 10–15% of British state revenue (Rappaport 2017).

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Trans-Siberian tea caravans
description: Canton — starting point for 6,000-mile overland caravan.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Trans-Siberian tea caravans
description: Ulaanbaatar — Mongolian steppe crossing.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Trans-Siberian tea caravans
description: Irkutsk — Siberian staging post, 16-month caravan total.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Trans-Siberian tea caravans
description: Moscow — arrival. Russian 'chai' derives from overland Mandarin 'cha'.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: British imperial shift (China → India)
description: Canton — pre-1842 chokehold and the lead-up to the Opium Wars.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: British imperial shift (China → India)
description: London — after losing Chinese monopoly pricing, Britain plants its own supply in India.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: British imperial shift (China → India)
description: Assam India — massive plantations with indentured labour from tribal communities in central India (Sharma 2011).

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: British imperial shift (China → India)
description: Darjeeling India — high-altitude Himalayan foothill plantations produce muscatel flavor at 6,000 ft.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: British imperial shift (China → India)
description: Ceylon (Sri Lanka) — British convert coffee plantations to tea after 1869 coffee-rust epidemic.

title: LAYER 2 — Language Diffusion: 'cha' path (overland)
description: Mandarin 'chá' — overland routes via Silk Road carry this word.

title: LAYER 2 — Language Diffusion: 'cha' path (overland)
description: Mongolia — 'tsai'.

title: LAYER 2 — Language Diffusion: 'cha' path (overland)
description: Russia — 'chai'.

title: LAYER 2 — Language Diffusion: 'cha' path (overland)
description: Hindi/Urdu — 'chai'. Overland via Silk Road through Central Asia.

title: LAYER 2 — Language Diffusion: 'cha' path (overland)
description: Persia — 'chay'. Overland trade into Arabic and Turkish.

title: LAYER 2 — Language Diffusion: 'te' path (maritime)
description: Min-Chinese (Fujian coast) 'té' — the sea traders' word.

title: LAYER 2 — Language Diffusion: 'te' path (maritime)
description: Dutch — 'thee'. Picked up from Fujianese sailors.

title: LAYER 2 — Language Diffusion: 'te' path (maritime)
description: English — 'tea'.

title: LAYER 2 — Language Diffusion: 'te' path (maritime)
description: French — 'thé'. Maritime trade path imports the Min word.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: China — 46% of world production; still origin + largest producer.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: India — 23% of world production. Assam + Darjeeling + Nilgiris.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: Kenya — 8% of world production, world's largest tea exporter by volume.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: Sri Lanka — 6% of world production.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: London — historical center of wholesale tea auctions.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: New York — modern specialty tea import hub.
